Last week (Jun 8 to Jun 14) saw conversions drop sharply to just 6, pushing CPA to $46, which is well above the 8-week trend where most weeks sat between $5 and $16. Clicks and spend actually recovered from the prior week's low (Jun 1 to Jun 7), so the traffic was there, but very little of it converted.

There is no strong positive story to tell from last week on conversions or CPA. The best angle is that clicks bounced back to 669 (up from 466 the week before) and spend recovered to $278, so the account is still generating traffic volume. But the conversion drop is the dominant story and worth flagging.

---

Here is the 8-week table with a summary on top:

**The account delivered solid conversion volumes through most of May, with CPA as low as $5 in the week of May 18. Last week (Jun 8 to Jun 14) was a sharp outlier: only 6 conversions recorded at a CPA of $46, despite clicks recovering to 669, which is worth investigating before drawing conclusions.**

*(HTML table below for Gmail use)*

The data as a quick reference:

| Week | Clicks | Spend | Conversions | CPA |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Apr 20 - Apr 26 | 617 | $263 | 35 | $7 |
| Apr 27 - May 3 | 762 | $318 | 35 | $9 |
| May 4 - May 10 | 751 | $319 | 20 | $16 |
| May 11 - May 17 | 767 | $323 | 29 | $11 |
| May 18 - May 24 | 718 | $306 | 59 | $5 |
| May 25 - May 31 | 871 | $377 | 30 | $13 |
| Jun 1 - Jun 7 | 466 | $194 | 25 | $8 |
| Jun 8 - Jun 14 | 669 | $278 | 6 | $46 |

**Last week call-out:** The conversion drop from 25 to 6 week on week is the story here, not a positive one. Before attributing it to a campaign issue, it is worth checking whether there was a tracking change, a landing page problem, or a form issue on the website during that week. The clicks are there at a reasonable volume, so the demand side looks fine. Want me to pull the search terms or campaign breakdown for Jun 8 to Jun 14 to dig into where the drop came from?
